items.title: Define a memory entry schema in packages/core/src/memory/types.ts
items.description: Foundation type definition that all other memory-wiring items depend on. Without the schema and writeMemoryEntry helper, ranks 2–6 cannot proceed. Unblocks 6 downstream items making this the single highest-leverage action in the sprint.

items.title: Wire CycleLogger.logCycleComplete() to write memory entry with type=cycle-outcome
items.description: First production write path into the memory system; seeding cycle-outcome entries is prerequisite for cross-cycle learning to have any data at all. Targeted test path (cycle 88026e07) reduces implementation ambiguity.

items.title: Wire gate phase handler to write memory entry of type=gate-verdict
items.description: Gate verdicts are the highest-signal learning data the system produces; capturing rationale and critical findings enables the audit phase (rank 5) to make meaningfully different decisions in the next cycle. Depends on rank-1 schema only.

items.title: Wire review phase handler to write memory entries of type=review-finding
items.description: Review findings (MAJOR/CRITICAL severity) are the raw seeds that the audit phase will read back on the next cycle; capturing them now means every future cycle benefits from accumulated reviewer knowledge. Depends on rank-1 schema only.

items.title: Update audit phase handler to inject recent memory entries into audit agent prompt
items.description: This is the cross-cycle learning closure: ranks 2–4 write, rank 5 reads. Without this injection the memory system is write-only and produces no value whatsoever. Logically depends on ranks 2–4 having populated entries to read back.

items.title: Update execute phase handler to inject relevant memory entries into each item's agent prompt
items.description: Personalizes each agent's context with tag-filtered past failures, improving execution quality per item without architectural changes. Natural companion to rank 5 using the same read infrastructure established by rank 1.

items.title: fix memory leak in compression module
items.description: An active memory leak in a production module is a stability risk that compounds over long-running autonomous cycles and confounds cost and performance anomaly signals. Tagged 'fix' with P1 priority; resolving it early removes a confounding variable.

items.title: Fix the /org dashboard route — verify org graph endpoint returns real delegation data
items.description: The /org graph is a primary operational view for understanding agent delegation; currently broken or empty. Well-scoped fix with explicit file targets (org/+page.svelte and v5/org-graph.ts) making it low-risk to execute autonomously.

items.title: Fix the /agents dashboard route — verify agent listing shows real agents from YAML files
items.description: Agent visibility is prerequisite for operators managing the autonomous loop; a broken agent listing undermines trust in system state. Scoped to a single route and a known YAML source directory.

items.title: Complete the /runner dashboard route — fix MODEL_TIERS undefined errors, wire agent dispatch form
items.description: Svelte-check compile errors indicate this route is broken at the type level; fixing it restores the ability to dispatch agents from the dashboard, which is central to v6.4+ autonomous loop UX. High confidence because the failures are already surfaced by static analysis.

items.title: Wire the /approvals dashboard page to GET/POST /api/v5/cycles/:id/approval endpoints
items.description: The approval workflow is the human-in-the-loop gate for autonomous sprints that exceed budget; without a working approvals UI operators must resort to CLI. Backend endpoints already exist (v6.7.4), making this a frontend wiring task with added scope for the SSE-triggered global modal.

items.title: Fix the /api/v5/memory backend to read new .agentforge/memory/*.jsonl files
items.description: Once ranks 1–6 write JSONL memory entries, the existing memory API must be updated to serve them; the current implementation reads the wrong file format and will return stale or empty data. Logically follows the write-path items and unblocks the dashboard memory view.

items.title: Fix the /search dashboard route — wire to existing backend endpoints for sessions, cycles, agents
items.description: Search returning no results regardless of query is a usability blocker for any operator trying to locate cycles, sessions, or agents. Broad impact across all dashboard content types justifies prioritization over polish items.

items.title: Update /memory dashboard page to render live memory feed with type chips, source links, and search
items.description: Operationalizing the memory system requires a UI that lets humans inspect what the system has learned; filtering and expandable entries are essential for debugging learning regressions. Depends on rank 1 schema and rank 12 API fix.

items.title: cache database queries to reduce latency from 500ms to <100ms
items.description: Concrete, measurable performance target (500ms to <100ms) with a clear implementation path (query caching). Directly benefits cycle throughput in the autonomous loop where DB queries run on every phase. No dependencies.

items.title: Wire up the /memory dashboard page to real backend with search box and agent filter
items.description: Extends rank-14 with search and agent-filter UX on the same page. Ranked below rank-14 because the core rendering must exist first; this adds discoverability features on top. Depends on rank-14 completing the base wiring.

items.title: Wire up the /flywheel dashboard page to compute real metrics from cycles and sprints
items.description: Flywheel health is a key management view for the autonomous loop; static gauges give no actionable information. Computing real metrics (meta-learning rate, autonomy score, velocity) requires aggregating across cycles and sprints — moderate scope but high operational value.

items.title: Make sprint cards on /sprints page clickable — wire onclick handler to /sprints/{version}
items.description: Trivially small fix (add onclick handler to a single Svelte file) with meaningful UX impact; the detail route already exists. Extremely high confidence given minimal scope and no dependencies.

items.title: add health check endpoint to dashboard API
items.description: A health check endpoint is foundational operational infrastructure that enables load balancers, monitors, and CI pipelines to verify liveness before dispatching work. Low cost, no dependencies, and a clear acceptance criterion.

items.title: Add tests/integration/memory-flow.test.ts — full cycle memory write and cross-cycle read
items.description: End-to-end integration test for the entire memory wiring sprint validates the learn-loop in CI and catches regressions across all 6 write/read paths. Should run after ranks 1–6 are complete so it validates real behavior rather than mocking the whole system.
